mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day forward return in periods of stagnant industrial production (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day forward return in periods of stable unemployment (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a weakening supply environment that boosts housing demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 60-day forward return in periods of stable housing starts (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day forward return in periods of stable federal funds rate (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 2.5% signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day forward return in periods of stagnant real GDP (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in initial jobless claims above 250,000 signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day forward return in periods of stable initial jobless claims (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening supply environment that boosts housing demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 60-day forward return in periods of stable industrial production (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2.5% signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day forward return in periods of stagnant real disposable personal income (t-test p < 0.05).
